reasoning: The base rate for a stock price to increase over a one-week horizon is slightly positive (historically ~53-55% for broad market, but CNC's trend analysis outputs 0.60 based on 0.53 from linear model but boosted by seasonality). CNC has fallen precipitously recently – down from $43 in early March to $32.0 on March 27, 2026. This drop was triggered by concerning comments about ACA challenges at a healthcare conference in mid-March, though they also reaffirmed 2026 guidance. A stock that has dropped rapidly and is well beneath all analyst price targets ($41 to $49) has a slightly better than 50/50 chance of a technical bounce (dead-cat bounce) or stabilization in the following week, since extreme fear starts to taper unless new negative events emerge. On the other hand, momentum is firmly downward, as the price dropped almost 5% on Friday, March 27 alone. I will moderate the 0.60 yfinance anchor downward because downward momentum in individual stocks often persists in the short-term before bottoming, but remain slightly above 0.5 due to the severe oversold conditions (cash flow yield is 26.8% per recent articles). Thus, I assign 53%.
